---
title: "Conclusion"
---

Based on the analysis, the power plant on Dauphin Island, Alabama is likely to experience reduced operating capacity due to elevated water temperatures. Recent temperature data, coupled with projections indicating continued long-term warming trends, suggest that these constraints will persist, particularly during peak summer months. Accordingly, the plant should proactively implement operational and efficiency measures to maintain optimal energy output under reduced-capacity conditions. Absent evidence of declining temperature trends, seasonal capacity limitations should be treated as an ongoing planning assumption.

## Recommendations

1.  Upgrade the heat exchangers and schedule regular maintenance before the summer months.

2.  Harvest cooler water from deeper in the gulf.

::: callout-warning
Harvesting cooler water is not the preferred adjustment to make, due to the fact that the depths around Dauphin Island reach less than 30 feet. According to @NOAAWaterTemp, significant changes in water temperature isn't observed until a depth of 200 feet. When looking at the @NCEI atlas, there would need to be about 50 miles worth of pipe laid to be able to access the cooler water that would help solve this issue.
:::
